title: FaithfulnessReport
description: Output of the `faithfulness` command: the axis (c) claim-faithfulness report (D11, D16, D21). Verdicts are exactly supported, partial, contradicted, insufficient-passage. 'insufficient-passage' is the renamed 'unverified_no_fulltext'; the old string is retired and must not appear in code, output, or docs. Every non-abstaining verdict is anchored on at least one stable passage ID from the D21 index, so a claim can always be traced to the exact passage (with page coordinates) that justified it. When the document is not 'full-text' on axis (d), the claim degrades to abstract level with verdict 'insufficient-passage' and MUST NOT be emitted as clean. 'insufficient-passage' is abstention, and the eval reports it on a risk-coverage curve so abstaining is priced rather than hidden inside accuracy.

protocol_version.description: Version of the axis (c) decision rules in force for this report.

method.description: Which faithfulness method produced the verdicts. M2 ships 'lexical-bm25' only (BM25 retrieval plus overlap heuristics); M3 layers richer claim anchoring on the same verdict vocabulary.

document.description: The document the claims were checked against, and how deep the available evidence went.

doc_id.description: Identifier of the indexed document, canonically the lowercased DOI.

doc_hash.description: Content hash of the extracted canonical text. Part of every passage ID, so a re-fetched and changed source produces new IDs and stale evidence is detectable.

accessibility.description: Axis (d) verdict for this document. Anything other than 'full-text' forces every claim to 'insufficient-passage'.

parser_version.description: Parser version that produced the passages. Changing it intentionally changes passage IDs (D21) and therefore invalidates cached verdicts.

source_response_hashes.description: Snapshots the document text was derived from.

claim_id.description: Stable identifier of the claim within the run.

claim.description: The claim text as submitted, verbatim.

clean.description: True only when the verdict rests on retrieved full-text passages. Always false for 'insufficient-passage': an abstention is never emitted as a clean result (D11).

score.description: Method-specific support score, 0.0 to 1.0. For the M2 lexical baseline this is the normalized overlap score of the best supporting passage.

reason.description: Human-readable justification naming the rule that fired, for example 'no full text: axis (d) accessibility is abstract-only'.

evidence.description: Passage anchors backing the verdict. Required non-empty for supported, partial, and contradicted: every such verdict is anchored on a stable passage ID. May be empty ONLY for 'insufficient-passage', which is precisely the case where no adequate passage exists.

passageAnchor.description: A pointer into the D21 passage index. The full passage shape lives in passage.schema.json; this anchor carries the fields needed to locate and re-verify the passage without re-reading the document.

bm25_score.description: BM25 rank score of this passage for the claim query. Comparable only within one document and one query.

text.description: The passage text, echoed for human review. Never the anchor: the passage_id is.

relation.description: How this passage relates to the claim: it may support it or contradict it. A 'contradicted' verdict must carry at least one anchor with relation 'contradicting'.

coverage.description: Fraction of claims that received a non-abstaining verdict, 0.0 to 1.0. The complement of abstention_rate.

abstention_rate.description: Fraction of claims answered 'insufficient-passage', 0.0 to 1.0. Reported alongside accuracy so abstention is visible and priced (the risk-coverage curve in evals/run_axes.py).

faithfulnessVerdict.description: Axis (c) verdict, exactly four states. 'supported': the passages entail the claim. 'partial': the passages support part of the claim, or support it with weaker qualifiers than claimed. 'contradicted': at least one passage states the opposite. 'insufficient-passage': no passage adequate to decide, including every case where full text was unavailable (the renamed 'unverified_no_fulltext'). This is abstention and is never clean.

passageId.description: Stable passage identifier per D21: hash(document content hash + section path + character offsets + parser version), hex encoded, optionally prefixed with 'sha256:'.

pageCoords.description: Page rectangles the passage occupies in the source PDF, in PDF user-space points with the origin at the top left. Empty or null for HTML sources, which have no pagination.
